name: sdlc-workflow
description: Complete SDLC workflow with integrated code review, unified design system, DiÃ¡taxis documentation, analysis, observability, and git lifecycle. 32 lifecycle commands (10 stages + wf-design [22 sub-commands] + wf-next + wf-status + wf-resume + wf-sync + wf-announce + wf-amend + wf-extend + wf-ideate + wf-how + wf-quick + wf-rca + wf-investigate + wf-discover + wf-instrument + wf-experiment + wf-benchmark + wf-profile + wf-skip + wf-close) with branch-aware git operations, wf-validate pre-write hook, 31 review commands, 7 aggregate reviews, 15 skills (design dispatcher + imagegen + 4 analysis + 7 DiÃ¡taxis + wf-profile), and setup-wide-logging command. wf-how routes across five modes: quick code answers, codebase exploration (parallel Explore agents + synthesis), deep web research (6â€“8 agents, 200+ sources), workflow artifact explanation, and findings explanation â€” with optional DiÃ¡taxis output. wf-plan includes reuse scan: Explore sub-agent 1 searches the wider codebase for existing utilities and capabilities before planning new implementations. v8.14: four standalone workflow entry points â€” wf-hotfix, wf-update-deps, wf-docs, wf-refactor. v8.17: wf-quick â€” compressed planning workflow. v8.18: wf-rca â€” read-only root-cause analysis with three parallel diagnosis sub-agents. v8.19: wf-investigate â€” investment discovery; parallel ROI-ranked candidate survey. v8.20: wf-discover â€” problem validation; external competitor/user/market research, build/do-not-build recommendation. v8.21: wf-instrument â€” observability augmentation; dark path detection + signal design, writes 04b-instrument.md. v8.22: wf-experiment â€” experiment design augmentation; hypothesis extraction, A-B/flag/canary design with metrics and rollback criteria, writes 04c-experiment.md. v8.23: wf-benchmark â€” two-mode perf wrapper; baseline before implement, compare after; regression tripwires at >10% CPU / >25% memory. v8.24: wf-profile skill + command â€” static + dynamic profiling via Node --cpu-prof, Go pprof, py-spy; standalone artifact at .ai/profiles/<run-id>/. v8.25: wf-skip â€” skip a stage without running it; stub artifact keeps downstream prerequisites satisfied. v8.26: wf-close â€” archive a workflow at any stage; five close reasons; 99-close.md with completed/incomplete summary and manual cleanup checklist. v8.27: Unified wf-design â€” 14 standalone design-* skills and 5 wf-design* commands collapsed into a single wf-design command (22 sub-commands) backed by a skills/design/ dispatcher + 24 reference docs. New imagegen skill with capability waterfall (built-in image_gen â†’ gpt-image-2 codex exec â†’ nano-banana-pro Gemini API â†’ text fallback). Imagegen probes in shape and craft sub-commands. Parallel codebase inspection sub-agents (token scanner, framework detector, context loader, surface ranger) before design work. Register-aware brand vs product divergence in all reference docs. Fixed: colorize side-stripe border anti-pattern, outdated animation-avoidance stance in animate/optimize/polish. v8.28: SDLC-aware sub-command routing for wf-design. craft splits by mode (workflow â†’ 02c-craft.md visual contract â†’ routes to /wf-implement; freestanding â†’ writes code). Stage-gated sub-commands: audit/critique require implement-or-later; 15 transformation sub-commands cannot run before implement. Transformations register as augmentations and write design-notes/<sub>-<timestamp>.md. Mutation gate distinguishes code/artifact/context/read-only types. v8.29: Closed the artifact-consumption gap â€” post-design stages (wf-slice, wf-plan, wf-implement, wf-verify, wf-review, wf-handoff, wf-ship, wf-retro) now consume design artifacts (02b-design.md, 02c-craft.md, design-notes/*, 07-design-audit.md, 07-design-critique.md). All stages that read augmentations: list now handle every type (instrument/experiment/benchmark/design-*) with type-specific behavior: implement adds instrumentation signals + experiment wiring as it codes; verify re-runs type-specific checks (axe-core for harden, perf for optimize, cohort tests for experiment, compare-mode for benchmark); handoff/ship translate every augmentation type to user-language changelog entries. Visual contract enforcement: 05-implement-<slice>.md gains a Â§ Visual Contract Honored section listing per-fidelity-item disposition; 06-verify-<slice>.md gains a Â§ Augmentation Verification section with per-augmentation pass/regression. Workflow-type awareness added to wf-implement/wf-verify/wf-review: compressed mode (workflow-type: quick â†’ reads 01-quick.md) and forwarded mode (workflow-type: rca/investigate â†’ reads 01-rca.md/01-investigate.md as rich context behind the synthesized 02-shape.md). Fixes a critical broken contract where wf-quick produced 01-quick.md but wf-implement only knew how to read 02-shape.md + 04-plan.md. v8.30: Per-slice review artifacts. `wf-review` now writes `07-review-<slice-slug>.md` (master verdict per slice) and `07-review-<slice-slug>-<command>.md` (per-command sub-reviews) instead of a single workflow-wide `07-review.md`/`07-review-<command>.md`. Running review on a second slice no longer overwrites the first slice's review â€” every reviewed slice keeps its own master verdict and per-command findings on disk, making 07 consistent with the per-slice naming already used by `03-slice-<slug>.md` through `06-verify-<slug>.md`. `wf-handoff` now parses each per-slice review's `verdict` and `metric-findings-blocker` frontmatter and refuses to proceed if any slice in scope has unresolved blockers, listing the offending slice slug(s). Consumers updated: `wf-implement` reviews mode (slice-scoped, accepts explicit slice arg); `wf-amend from-review` (per-slice, aggregates siblings on cross-slice spec errors); `wf-extend from-review` (globs every per-slice review since missing-capability findings often span slices); `wf-retro`, `wf-ship`, `wf-how findings` (glob across all per-slice review files); `wf-skip review` (slice-scoped stub); `wf-status` and `wf-verify` frontmatter `refs.review` updated; `wf-handoff` and `wf-ship` frontmatter changed `refs.review` to `refs.reviews: [...]`. v8.30.1: wf-implement now loads design references named in `02b-design.md`'s YAML frontmatter `recommended-references: [...]` array and reads each as `skills/design/reference/<name>.md` for read-only implementation rationale. Closes a wiring gap from v8.29 where the brief's recommended-references section was produced by `/wf-design shape` but never consumed downstream. Fixes typo in `reference/shape.md` (`typography.md` â†’ `typeset.md`) and mandates frontmatter mirror so the array is machine-parseable. v8.31.0: **No-optional-artifacts policy.** Every lifecycle stage's

across `wf-slice`, `wf-plan`, `wf-implement`, `wf-verify`, `wf-review`, `wf-handoff`, `wf-ship`, and `wf-retro`. A new uniform Workflow rules bullet states the policy: if any file listed in the row exists on disk, the stage MUST read it and the stage's output MUST honor it. Existence is optional; consumption is required. Silent omission is now a contract violation rather than a permitted shortcut. Per-row text was strengthened with explicit MUST language for every conditional input (e.g., implement now states `02b-design.md` register/anti-goals MUST be honored; verify states `02c-craft.md` mock fidelity inventory MUST be re-verified; ship states every augmentation MUST get a changelog entry). `wf-slice` now consumes both `02b-design.md` and `02c-craft.md` (slice was previously omitted from consuming 02c entirely). For both 02b and 02c in slice, distinct states/surfaces MUST become slice boundaries â€” or `03-slice.md` `## Slice Strategy` MUST justify any grouping with one sentence per state/surface. Closes a stage-skip gap where `/wf-design craft` (which routes to `/wf-implement`) could leave a finalized visual contract that slice would ignore if invoked afterward, and uniformly closes the broader gap where any downstream stage could silently ignore a present artifact under the prior
